all.summary: Add ability to search for sessions
Create GraphQL Client for Energizer APIs
Remove delete session from device session page
Handle large device session downloads (10k animals) with retry and FNn fallback
Handle Eziweight7i RFID / EID column name like in Datalink
Eziweight7i Wrong column order in export
Refactor and Connect Energizer Bluetooth Commands with UI
Update Energizer Detail Page UI to Match Figma Design
In app navigation hidden behind phone navigation
XRS2i don't download sessions on reconnection
Clarify session cards labels and hide farm sessions card
Add Terms an Codition and Privacy to sign up
Select Farm page remembers what I previously typed.  If it does this, it should present it in the search box when I go to search
Add disconnect button when I am connected to a device
Connection error related to permissions shown, even though I have granted permission
VID not populating on session summary screen for an animal
Order sessions by date recorded descending
Refresh session list while connected to device
Support for 0 animals in session list
Add 'Coming Soon' message dialog to any items that are not expected to be functioning right now
Device disconnects if I navigate around app
Handle user role in api call
check if app is online (connected to internet)
Update Intercom Integration to Identify Logged-in Users
Send sessions to DML once logged in
Create “Sessions” widget on the app homepage
New Transaction type for NLIS - Sighted Livestock
Prompt user to migrate data from Mihub at first login
Integrate Intercom into the app (Anonymous Support + Future Identification)

notes.total: 96 tickets across 7 sprints is a healthy volume for the quarter, averaging ~14 per sprint with no signs of overload.
notes.completed: All 96 tickets completed — perfect execution against the quarter plan.
notes.completion_rate: 100% completion rate demonstrates excellent sprint discipline and realistic scoping throughout Q3.
notes.releases_shipped: 7 releases shipped (1 per sprint on average) shows consistent delivery cadence, though last release dated December suggests post-quarter tagging.
notes.oos_total: Zero out-of-sprint additions across the entire quarter — exemplary sprint planning and scope protection.
notes.oos_open: No unplanned work left open — clean quarter closure.
notes.type_split: Healthy mix with 83% stories, 9% tasks, and only 7% bugs (7 total) indicates strong product quality.
notes.avg_releases: Median and mean of 1.0 releases per sprint reflects steady, predictable shipping rhythm.
notes.time_logged: Only 8 hours logged against 2,522 estimated hours — time tracking is effectively non-existent and undermines planning data.
notes.estimate_accuracy: Cannot calculate accuracy with near-zero time logging; estimation process exists but tracking discipline does not.
notes.no_estimate: Zero tickets shipped without estimates — excellent estimation hygiene across all work.
notes.rollover: Zero rollover tickets — every sprint closed cleanly with no carryover debt.
notes.cycle_time: Median 15 days (mean 18.6) is higher than ideal but reasonable for a story-heavy quarter; outliers added ~4 days to the average.
notes.assignee_workload: Giuseppe carried 41% of tickets (39), with workload well-distributed across Gianni (25), Fabrizio (23), and Nikola (8); no single-point-of-failure risk.
